input.prompt:
I have a .NET 10 CoreCLR web API running on Linux. I need to configure
automatic crash dump collection so that when the app crashes, a full
dump is written to /var/dumps. Show me the environment variables to set
and how to verify the configuration.

<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:02:06.902Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET 10 CoreCLR web API running on Linux. I need to configure

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET 10 CoreCLR web API running on Linux. I need to configure
automatic crash dump collection so that when the app crashes, a full
dump is written to /var/dumps. Show me the environment variables to set
and how to verify the configuration.

data.content:
Here's how to configure automatic crash dump collection for .NET on Linux:

### Environment Variables

```bash
# Enable crash dumps and set the output path
export DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1
export DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4          # 4 = Full heap dump
export DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/var/dumps/coredump.%p.%t
```

**`DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` values:**
| Value | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| 1 | Mini | Small dump with stacks + limited info |
| 2 | Heap | Includes GC heap |
| 3 | Triage | Minimal, stripped-down dump |
| 4 | Full | Full process memory (largest, most complete) |

`%p` = PID, `%t` = timestamp — prevents overwriting on repeated crashes.

### Setup & Verification

```bash
# 1. Create the dump directory with appropriate permissions
sudo mkdir -p /var/dumps
sudo chmod 1777 /var/dumps   # or chown to your app's user

# 2. Disable the OS core pattern so it doesn't conflict
#    (the .NET runtime writes dumps itself via createdump)
echo '|/bin/false' | sudo tee /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern

# 3. Verify the variables are visible to your process
dotnet --info   # confirms runtime version
env | grep DOTNET_Dbg

# 4. Test by sending SIGABRT to a running app
kill -6 <PID>

# 5. Confirm a dump was written
ls -lh /var/dumps/
```

### For systemd-managed services

Add the variables to your unit file:

```ini
[Service]
Environment=DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1
Environment=DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4
Environment=DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/var/dumps/coredump.%p.%t
```

Then `sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl restart your-app`.

### Analyzing the dump

```bash
# Install dotnet-dump globally
dotnet tool install -g dotnet-dump

# Open the dump
dotnet-dump analyze /var/dumps/coredump.<pid>.<timestamp>

# Useful SOS commands inside the analyzer:
#   clrstack      – managed call stacks
#   pe            – print last exception
#   dumpheap      – heap summary
```

> **Note:** The older `COMPlus_` prefix still works but `DOTNET_` is the canonical prefix since .NET 6+.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about configuring automatic crash dump collection for a .NET 10 CoreCLR application running on Linux. This is a knowledge-based question about .NET runtime configuration, not a code change task. I can answer this directly.
